$comment: Cedar Storybook documentation style guide. Human-authored. Read-only to automation. Changes require PR + ADR. Must stay in sync with .storybook/_content.scss.

principles.$comment: The three rules a human or agent should check any new style decision against.
principles.tokenFirst: Every value must trace to a Cedar token. No hardcoded px, colors, or font names.
principles.docSiteParityRule: When in doubt, match cedar.rei.com. Open content-styles.scss and check.
principles.semanticNotVisual: Reference tokens by their semantic name (cdr-color-text-primary), not their visual description (dark gray).

wrapperClass.$comment: Every story that renders documentation content must use this class. The agent must emit it.
wrapperClass.name: cdr-doc-content

typography.$comment: Element-to-mixin mapping. Agent uses this to generate story prose templates.
typography.note: Never use $cdr-color-text-brand (green) for documentation headings. That is a brand accent, not a content hierarchy signal.

knownWrongPatterns.$comment: Patterns the agent must flag and correct. This is the diff between what Windsurf generated and what is correct.

headingFontFamily.reason: Doc site uses sans (Graphik) for headings. Serif (Stuart) is editorial body copy only.

headingColor.reason: Brand green is an accent. Documentation headings use primary text color.

tableHeaderBackground.reason: Semantic table token exists. Use it.

tableRowBorder.reason: Semantic table token exists. Use it.

tablePadding.reason: Doc site uses one-x on all sides. Half-x is cramped.

listStyle.reason: Doc site uses em dash list markers.

linkStyle.correct: full link styles with hover, active, visited states
linkStyle.reason: Links must handle rest, hover, active, visited states. Post-NX: use cdr-link-base-mixin.

spacing.$comment: Approved margins for documentation elements. These match content-styles.scss exactly.

calloutComponents.$comment: Semantic wrappers for special content blocks. Use these instead of ad-hoc styling.

releaseNotes.$comment: Release notes are documentation content. They use cdr-doc-content. No additional class needed unless a release-note-specific override is required.

prohibitedPatterns.$comment: Patterns the agent must never emit in story SCSS or story template styles.
list: hardcoded px, rem, or em values where a space token exists
hardcoded hex or rgb color values
inline style attributes on documentation wrapper elements
custom CSS classes not registered in this file's calloutComponents

tokenGaps.$comment: Tokens referenced in content-styles.scss that do not exist as SCSS variables in the docsite theme build. These are documented here so humans and agents know the gap exists.

agentInstructions.$comment: Loaded by the agent before any story generation or style validation. These are the enforcement rules.
beforeGenerating: Read this file completely before writing any story template or SCSS.
Check every heading against typography.note — never use $cdr-color-text-brand.
Check every table against knownWrongPatterns before emitting table styles.
Every link must include hover, active, and visited states.
Every list must use em dash (\2014) list markers.
validationChecks: Does every story that renders prose content have class='cdr-doc-content' on its wrapper?
Does any heading use $cdr-color-text-brand or $cdr-font-family-serif? If yes, flag and correct.
Does any table use $cdr-color-background-primary instead of $cdr-color-background-table-header? If yes, flag and correct.
Does any link omit hover/active/visited states? If yes, flag and correct.
Does any hardcoded px value appear where a Cedar space token exists? If yes, flag and correct.
